name: Goat's Cheese Salad with Sweet and Sour Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the dressing by combining the vinegars and seasoning. Then vigorously whisk in the combined oils to create a creamy emulsion.
2. Chop the tomatoes very finely, add them to the dressing, and let it stand on one side.
3. Peel the onions and slice them into rings, no more than 1/4 inch thick, preferably finer.
4. Heat oil and butter in a frying pan over a moderate flame until the butter bubbles. Add onion rings and stir until well coated. Cook for about 15 minutes, stirring frequently until the onions have softened and taken on color. Remove from heat and set aside.
5. Slice the goat's cheeses into halves. Turn the grill to high. Quickly dress salad leaves with a little dressing and divide between 6 plates. Place cheese halves on a baking tray, cut side up, and broil for about 5 minutes until golden brown flecks appear and the inside begins to melt. Return onions to heat and stir in sherry vinegar and sugar. Boil furiously for a couple of minutes until juices turn syrupy. Remove from heat and season with salt and pepper.
6. To serve, place a goat's cheese half on each plate with salad, then scatter sweet and sour onions over the top. Finally, drizzle another tablespoon of dressing over each plate to ensure good distribution of sun-dried tomatoes.
